concerns.text: The plan relies on a specific OSF dataset (https://osf.io/3k9r2/) containing experimental feedback manipulation data (feedback_cond, pre/post anxiety/rumination/self-efficacy). The 'Verified Datasets' block in research.md explicitly states: 'NOT (zip) from HuggingFace (Solidity code, irrelevant)' and 'OSF (parquet) from HuggingFace (OSF loglikelihood, unrelated to feedback)'. It further admits 'ANCOVA: NO verified source found'. The plan assumes the OSF URL is live and contains the exact schema, but the research artifact confirms no verified dataset matching the required experimental design (feedback manipulation + specific psych scales) exists in the provided verified list. Proceeding with this plan risks a fatal runtime failure or analysis of an irrelevant dataset if the OSF link is dead or mismatched.

The plan states 'Dataset must be subsetted if >7GB (unlikely for this study)' and assumes a dataset of 'approx. 100-500 rows'. However, the 'research.md' section 'Verified Datasets' confirms that no verified source for the required experimental data exists. The plan's feasibility is contingent on an unverified external URL (osf.io/3k9r2) which the research artifact explicitly flags as having 'NO verified source found' for the specific ANCOVA requirements. The analysis cannot run as written without a confirmed, accessible dataset matching the schema.
